data.content: The build confirms the file is generated (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler doesn't see it. The issue is that the `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. The compiler only compiles files it knows about.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` which generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is generated but the compiler doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group using `<ItemGroup>` with `<Compile Include="..." />` inside the target, after the file is generated.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>

data.content:
**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="..." />` item inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This is a common issue with code-generation targets — generating a file isn't enough; you must also add it to the `Compile` item group before `CoreCompile` runs.
